The new is_structured_combine gate fires (>1 non-grid tile AND >=1 non-reduction apply tile over the SAME extent) -> 1 reduction_fact, 1 seed.

welford.added: 2026-05-29 (v6 classified+DECLINED; v7 Band-C treatment IMPLEMENTED + seeded)

welford.the_non_pow2_bug_rootcause: The single-axis seed (pre-v7) FLOORED block 2 (normalize) to width 1 -> ~10-20x slower (G=0.05) AND was numerically WRONG at non-pow2 N. ROOT CAUSE confirmed by codegen: the Welford combine uses Tn=chunk.size(-1), which lowers to the tile constexpr _BLOCK_SIZE (NOT the masked valid count). When the combine tile = next_pow2(N) > N (masked, e.g. 2048 at N=1536) Tn=2048 != 1536 -> sum_x/Tn divides by the padded width -> err=0.69 FAILS allclose. The fix: size the combine tile as a power-of-2 DIVISOR of N so every chunk is FULL (no mask) -> Tn == true count. Helion also REJECTS a non-pow2 exact-N combine tile (PowerOfTwoFragment: 'must be a power of two, got 1536'), so 'exact-N tile' is impossible -> a pow2 divisor (512 for 1536) is the only correct option.
welford.seed: is_structured_combine branch: combine tile (block 1) = min(largest_pow2_div(N), STRUCTURED_COMBINE_CAP_BYTES//itemsize=2048 fp32); apply tile (block 2) = next_pow2(N) persistent (masked output write drops only invalid lanes -> correct); M_BLOCK at floor (16 at M=262144); num_warps per the existing rnumel ramp. Per shape: (1024)->[16,1024,1024]/w4; (1536)->[16,512,2048]/w8 (looped divisor combine, persistent normalize); (2048)->[16,2048,2048]/w8; (4096)->[16,2048,4096]/w8.
welford.default_config_behavior: deterministic default_config() = block_sizes=[16,16,16] (both tiles chunk-16) -> CORRECT (16 divides all in-sample N) but SLOW: G_default 0.51-0.54.
welford.status: WIN (v8 apply-tile cap): G_welford=0.9105 geomean (v7 was 0.894) vs default 0.526. do_bench median, fp32, GPU2/GPU3 (reproduced both). CORRECT at ALL 4 shapes incl the non-pow2 canary (262144,1536) AND prime N (1543/3079) AND non-pow2-with-looped-apply (3072/6144): maxabs<=8.6e-6, allclose rtol/atol=1e-3 PASS. v8 per-shape G: (1024)=0.996, (1536)=0.924, (2048)=0.988, (4096)=0.757 (v7 was 0.706). v8 seed per shape: (1024)->[16,1024,1024]/w4 (=v7), (1536)->[16,512,2048]/w8 (=v7), (2048)->[16,2048,2048]/w8 (=v7), (4096)->[16,4096,2048]/w8 (v7 was [16,2048,4096]: combine 2048->4096 full pow2-div, apply 4096-persist -> 2048-LOOPED). v8 CHANGE: apply tile = min(np2(N), STRUCTURED_APPLY_CAP_BYTES=8KiB) so wide-N apply LOOPS; combine cap COUPLED 8KiB->16KiB when apply looped (the looped apply frees the working set, making a full combine spill-safe). EVIDENCE (matched A/B, wf_apply_sweep+wf_v8_decision+wf_brief_repro): at (262144,4096) apply tile sweep 4096p/2048L/1024L/512L = G 0.704/0.722/0.703/0.688 -> apply-cap alone is +2.5%; full ~7% (0.722->0.757) needs the coupled combine-uncap; combine spill-safe ceiling 16KiB (wf_combine_largeN_safety: combine>16KiB regresses at N>=32768 even with looped apply). OVERSTATEMENT CORRECTED: the prior 'oracle reaches G=0.737/0.757 via a structure the seed doesn't explore' AND any 'seed/oracle=1.04' / '27% apply-tile win' are WRONG. v8 IS that structure (block_sizes ceiling G=0.757). The FRESH quick-autotune oracle reaches G=0.968 -- but a knob-isolation A/B (wf_oracle_knob_isolate.py) PROVES that is ENTIRELY autotuner-only codegen knobs (indexing=[..,tensor_descriptor,tensor_descriptor,..]+load_eviction_policies=['last','first',..]+range_multi_buffers/range_num_stages): the oracle's OWN block_sizes [16,2048,2048] at DEFAULT knobs = G=0.722 (=our bare A/B), adding ONLY those knobs lifts it 0.722->0.968; adding them to the v8 seed lifts 0.757->0.947. So beyond v8's block_sizes ceiling the residual is NOT seedable (same codegen-knob residual as the 8 grid-bound kernels), NOT a config the seed missed. is_structured_combine keying INERT for all 8 active kernels (18/18 byte-identical) AND for welford small-N (N<=2048 apply persistent -> byte-identical to v7; only N=4096 changes). Harness: _lab/harness/{wf_correct_configs,wf_clean_probe,wf_cap_decision,wf_final_seed,wf_oracle,wf_impl_verify,wf_no_regression_8,wf_predicate_check,wf_apply_sweep,wf_brief_repro,wf_v8_decision,wf_combine_cap_generalize,wf_combine_largeN_safety,wf_v8_correct_nonpow2_prime,wf_v8_smallN_byteidentical,wf_v8_seed_vs_oracle,wf_oracle_knob_isolate}.py

TRAP_seed_degraded_on_injection.finding: On ALL 4 shapes (rnumel>4096) the seed's reduction_loops=[None] (PERSISTENT) is silently flat-encoded to a LOOPED chunk of 4096 when injected into the autotuner. num_warps + block_sizes survive; the persistent lever does NOT.
TRAP_seed_degraded_on_injection.root_cause: ReductionLoopSpec._encode_flat_value (config_spec.py ~1799) maps None -> _flat_fragment.default() = min(next_pow2(rnumel),4096). unflatten restores None only if flat_int>=size_hint, false for rnumel>4096.
TRAP_seed_degraded_on_injection.proof: [None] codegen has no `for roffset` loop (1 tl.arange); [4096] codegen has the loop (2 tl.arange) -- different kernels. The Product-A bare-seed path (configs=[seed]) is UNAFFECTED (keeps [None]); only the autotuner-injection path degrades it.
TRAP_seed_degraded_on_injection.implication: Product-B wins are a LOWER BOUND -- the seed reaches gen0 carrying only its num_warps advantage, not its headline persistent lever. Fixing the ReductionLoopSpec round-trip should widen the early-budget gap. This is a registered-seed defect for the autotuner path; NOT a Product-A regression.
TRAP_seed_degraded_on_injection.open_lever: make ReductionLoopSpec round-trip None (sentinel that decodes back to persistent), or inject the persistent choice through a flat channel that survives. Worth doing before re-running Product B.

run_2026_05_29.verdict: Seeding shifts the convergence curve UP-and-LEFT on all 4 shapes. Headline time-to-95% win 1.5-1.8x on 3/4 shapes (rms_norm x2, sum); long_sum ~tie on time-to-target but big gen0 win (3.8x at g0; absolute latencies ~10us noise floor). Full-budget no-regression holds. Achieved with the persistent lever DEGRADED on injection -- a lower bound. Honest: it is NOT a cheaper full search; the win is reaching a good config sooner / being able to shrink the budget.

long_sum_(8,131072).note: REGRESSED on this metric but it is a NOISE-FLOOR + total-wallclock artifact, NOT a real loss. The 95% target = 0.0081ms (8.1us) sits in the ~7-10us tiny-M (M=8) noise floor where BOTH modes reach target via random LFBO dips; the wallclock-to-target is dominated by total search time. Post-fix the persistent seed makes LFBO compile MANY more neighbors (seeded total wall-clock 17.6-47s vs unseeded 14.6-40s -- the documented 'seeded full search is longer' caveat), so a lucky unseeded run trips the razor-thin 8.1us threshold sooner. Was already a near-tie pre-fix (1.04x). The HONEST long_sum claim is the PERF win (gen0 4.0x, Slice-1 gen1 1.45x), which the fix INCREASED.

total_full_search_wallclock_median_s._caveat: seed still makes the FULL search LONGER (LFBO explores around the better config). The Product-B value is gen0/early-budget PERF + time-to-target on shapes above the noise floor (rms_norm), NOT a cheaper full search.

Full-budget guardrail PASSES all 3.

test2_within_oracle_bundle.method: the DECISIVE isolation: take the verbatim oracle lever bundle (block_sizes=[4], reduction_loops=[128], num_warps=1, num_stages=5) and flip ONLY pid_type within it. If pid is real-but-coupled the win survives the flip; if flat ties/beats persistent in the bundle, pid is a passenger.
test2_within_oracle_bundle.result: flat BEATS persistent_interleaved WITHIN the oracle bundle at every shape+multiplier. flat/pi: rms_norm 32768x256 m1=0.51/m2=0.72/m4=0.87; sum 32768x256 m1=0.44/m2=0.68/m4=0.83; sum 8192x256 m1=0.59/m2=0.84/m4=0.95. The oracle bundle with FLAT (rms_norm 34.98us, sum 24.80us) is FASTER than the identical bundle with persistent_interleaved (40-69us).
test2_within_oracle_bundle.g_characterization: G=tc/lat (median-of-7): sum 8192x256 G_v6=0.99 G_oracleFlat=1.00 G_oraclePI2=0.86; sum 32768x256 G_v6=1.02 G_oracleFlat=1.02 G_oraclePI2=0.70; rms_norm 8192x256 G_v6=0.89 G_oracleFlat=1.00 G_oraclePI2=0.75; rms_norm 32768x256 G_v6=0.94 G_oracleFlat=0.97 G_oraclePI2=0.72. The ONLY real grid-bound headroom (rms_norm ~3-11%) comes from the M-block/looped-chunk/warps levers via FLAT pid; adding persistent_interleaved DESTROYS it.

codegen_knob_stage1_pid_type.mechanism: flat launches one program per M-block (grid=ceil(M/M_BLOCK)) -> full grid occupancy for free. persistent_interleaved launches only num_sms*mult programs and serially loops virtual PIDs; for a tiny-rnumel reduction each row's work is small, so the serialized virtual-pid loop adds overhead with NO occupancy benefit (the SMs were already saturated by flat's large grid). PROOF: at extreme num_sm_multiplier=32 (persistent grid 132*32=4224 ~= flat grid 32768/4=8192) persistent_interleaved finally just TIES flat (sum 32768x256 flat/pi mult8=0.87, mult16=0.97, mult32=1.01, mult64=1.00) -- it degenerates into flat's near-one-program-per-block launch and never beats it. There is NO grid-occupancy physics win here; flat is already grid-saturated for these reductions.
codegen_knob_stage1_pid_type.g_premise_not_reproduced: The brief's 'sum (8192,256) G=1.58' does NOT reproduce in matched do_bench: G_v6(sum 8192x256)=0.99 (already ~tc) and G_v6(sum 32768x256)=1.02 (already beats tc). sum has essentially NO Product-A headroom at these shapes. The 30.6us autotuner-INTERNAL oracle number (vs my fair do_bench of the same bundle = 34.98us flat / 40-69us pid) is the autotuner's own timing, not a fair re-bench -- and even taken at face value, flat beats pid in the identical bundle.

This is the v2-confound pattern (a coupled-config win misattributed to one lever). DO NOT seed pid_type/num_sm_multiplier for these reductions. Future Stage-2 (indexing/eviction) and the FLAT oracle bundle's M-block/looped-chunk/warps levers are where the small grid-bound headroom actually lives.

explorer_confirmation.TD_mechanism: tensor_descriptor (TMA) is INERT unless the leading tiled dim has >=2 rows; the seed tiles rows at block_size=1 ([1] or [1,N]) so the load is not a 2D tile TMA targets -> stays pointer, codegen byte-identical. Only engages on welford (block [16,...]) where it OOMs (smem 524296 > 232448 limit). FUTURE: TD could be revisited ONLY via a coupled block-size co-design (wide multi-row tile + combine small enough for 232KB smem) — out of matched-lever knob scope.
explorer_confirmation.num_stages_mechanism: memory-bound reductions, no inner GEMM to overlap -> software pipelining yields nothing (within ±0.2% on low-noise shapes welford/rms 8192x8192/32768x256).
explorer_confirmation.verdict: CLOSE codegen-knob workstream as NULL (eviction+pid were the wins). long_sum few-row -> split-K/block-size workstream (deferred). Strong shapes (rms 8192x8192, sum 2048x16384, softmax 4096x4096) un-regressed.
